mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is a major component of GDP
failure_condition: If RSAFS growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over a 5-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic weakness, leading to reduced corporate profits and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by >0.5pp over 3 months, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of high inflation and rising federal funds rates indicates aggressive monetary tightening, which increases borrowing costs and reduces corporate valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Monetary policy aims to control inflation
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 shows positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of periods where both FEDFUNDS > 0.1% and CPIAUCSL > 230, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A strong rebound in housing starts from a low base indicates renewed confidence in the economy and future demand, stimulating related industries and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 10% month-over-month after being below 900 for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances during post-recession periods, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls signifies a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Employment growth is a key economic indicator
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward returns of the S&P 500 are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.1) during periods of positive PAYEMS growth over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production indicates weakening manufacturing activity and potential supply chain disruptions or reduced demand for goods, leading to lower corporate profits and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls by more than 1% month-over-month
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 45% of instances where INDPRO declines by >1% MoM, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate signals an accommodative monetary policy stance, which reduces the cost of capital for businesses and encourages investment, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Low interest rates stimulate economic activity
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher than average during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% over a 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of stagnant GDP growth and elevated unemployment signals a severe economic downturn or recession, leading to widespread corporate distress and significant negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Recessions are characterized by falling GDP and rising unemployment
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 35% of periods where both GDPC1 growth is <0.5% and UNRATE > 7.0%, the hypothesis is rejected.
